author: potuz
content: actually approach 2 is much more complicated because the actual gas limit anyway will have to be sent in the beacon block

author: potuz
content: not only cached in the state

author: potuz
content: it's probably a waste of time to write that PR

author: jtraglia
content: <@755590043632140352> in your PR, should we check the gas limit in `process_execution_payload_bid` too? Not sure it makes sense because I don't want there to be a dependency on proposer preferences in state transition.

author: jtraglia
content: Your changes here look good though. I did a simple variable rename.

author: jtraglia
content: I guess the EL would throw an exception here? So no need.

author: potuz
content: no no, it's completely offchain

author: jtraglia
content: Yes, I agree. I don't want to do that.

author: jtraglia
content: Just wanting to double check what would happen if the (malicious/buggy) proposer includes a bid where the gas limit is higher/lower than it should be. The EL would reject that, right?

author: jtraglia
content: <@586161934425128960> where will the target gas limit in payload attributes come from?

author: nflaig
content: same as `suggested_fee_recipient`, it's provided by the caller, the source is not defined in the spec but in practice it will be configured in the validator client and supplied to the beacon node when submitting the proposer preferences via beacon api

author: jtraglia
content: Hmm I meant from the non-proposer perspective. As a random node that's verifying the payload.

author: jtraglia
content: Would they use the gas limit from the bid?

author: nflaig
content: yeah, I don't think it's a good idea to get it from the proposer preferences

author: jtraglia
content: Agreed. And if it comes from the bid, we need to have appropriate checks (outside of gossip) for that.

author: jtraglia
content: Like how should the gas limit be checked for self-built payloads? <@755590043632140352>

author: potuz
content: what?

author: potuz
content: there's no consensus check in my PR

author: potuz
content: the PR is only about propagating bids compatible with the proposer's preferences

author: jtraglia
content: Yeah I'm saying there might need to be.

author: potuz
content: why?

author: jtraglia
content: Or is that going to be handled later?

author: potuz
content: why do we need to make it a consensus thing?

author: jtraglia
content: What happens in this situation? ^^^

author: potuz
content: we can make it a consensus thing, but it's more complicated for no reason

author: potuz
content: this is not a problem. if the gas limit is incorrect, then 1559 will check that

author: jtraglia
content: 1559 checked in the EL?

author: potuz
content: if the gas limit is correct from the point of view of the EL and incorrect wrt the proposer's preference's, that's fine

author: potuz
content: yes, the EL checks the actual consensus on the gas limit

author: potuz
content: the CL just checks that the builders are following the proposers preferences

author: potuz
content: there's no need to make it a consensus thing as we talked in the call. We can make it though if people want to, but it becomes more complicated and it doesn't give us anything

author: jtraglia
content: Okay yeah this sounds reasonable. There just needs to be something that prevents a proposer from doing something bad.

author: potuz
content: there's nothing bad in the proposer choosing a bid against it's own preferences

author: jtraglia
content: If `verify_and_notify_new_payload` throws an exception or returns false here, that's fine.

author: jtraglia
content: What if its bid.gas_limit is greater than the actual limit?

author: potuz
content: if the payload is actually bad, the EL will return INVALID

author: jtraglia
content: Cool yeah that's all I wanted to double check.

author: potuz
content: this doesn't change

author: nflaig
content: so what's the issue with your PR <@755590043632140352> ,seems quite elegant to me

author: nflaig
content: also closest to what we do today

author: potuz
content: it's fine <@586161934425128960> just shameful that I spoke something in ACDT and then did something else 5 minutes later

author: nflaig
content: it solves the issue of builder being able to control the gas limit and at the same time it keeps it out of consensus

author: nflaig
content: still dislike having EIP-1559 (EL code) in the CL spec

author: nflaig
content: but at least the algorithm is part of the spec now
